name: GPT-4
strengths: Strong general reasoning for its era
Kicked off the modern LLM product wave
weaknesses: Small context window
Expensive by modern standards
No native reasoning mode
notes: The model that defined the 2023 frontier.

name: GPT-4 Turbo
weaknesses: Noticeably lazier on long completions
Superseded quickly by GPT-4o
notes: Announced at OpenAI's first DevDay.

name: GPT-4o
strengths: Fast and cheap for its capability
Defined the mainstream chat experience of 2024
weaknesses: Weak at competition math and hard science
No extended reasoning
notes: OpenAI's first natively multimodal flagship.

name: o1
strengths: Large jump on math and science benchmarks
weaknesses: Slow and expensive
Reasoning tokens billed but hidden
notes: Full release of the o1 series (previewed September 2024).

name: GPT-4.1
weaknesses: API-only at launch
Not a reasoning model
notes: Workhorse non-reasoning line before GPT-5 unified the stack.

name: o3
strengths: 80% price cut in June 2025 made it a bargain
weaknesses: Higher hallucination rate than o1 on some evals
Slow at high reasoning effort
notes: Pricing reflects the June 2025 cut from $10/$40.

name: GPT-5
strengths: Unified router across fast and reasoning modes
Big drop in hallucination rate vs o3
weaknesses: Rocky launch (router issues, 4o removal backlash)
Personality seen as colder than 4o
notes: Scores shown for GPT-5 (thinking, high).

name: GPT-5.1
strengths: Adaptive reasoning (spends thinking only when needed)
weaknesses: Incremental gains over GPT-5 on hard benchmarks
notes: Shipped as Instant and Thinking variants.

name: Claude 3 Opus
strengths: First model to beat GPT-4 on major benchmarks
weaknesses: Very expensive
Slow
notes: Flagship of the Claude 3 generation.

name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet
strengths: Beloved for coding — powered the first agentic coding wave
weaknesses: Weak competition math
8K max output limiting for large diffs
notes: Scores reflect the upgraded October 2024 snapshot.

name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
strengths: First hybrid reasoning model (instant or extended thinking)
weaknesses: Extended-thinking scores require high token budgets
notes: GPQA/SWE-bench figures use extended thinking.

name: Claude Opus 4
strengths: Best coding model in the world at launch
weaknesses: Premium pricing
ASL-3 deployment safeguards added friction
notes: Released with Claude Sonnet 4 at the first Code with Claude conference.

name: Claude Sonnet 4
strengths: Matched Opus 4 on SWE-bench at 1/5 the price
weaknesses: Trails Opus on long-horizon agentic work

name: Claude Opus 4.1
weaknesses: Incremental release two days before GPT-5 stole the news cycle

name: Claude Sonnet 4.5
strengths: Best coding model in the world at launch
weaknesses: Mid-tier reasoning ceiling vs contemporaneous Opus-class models

name: Claude Haiku 4.5
strengths: Near-Sonnet-4.5 coding at a third of the price
weaknesses: Trails flagships on deep reasoning and long-horizon tasks
notes: Anthropic's current small model.

name: Claude Opus 4.5
strengths: First model past 80% on SWE-bench Verified
weaknesses: 200K context trailed rivals' 1M windows

name: Gemini 1.5 Pro
weaknesses: Mid-pack reasoning
Trailing coding performance

name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
strengths: Native tool use and image generation
weaknesses: Not frontier-level on hard reasoning
notes: Included as the price-performance outlier of its generation. SWE-bench Verified 51.8 is Google-reported using an agentic code-execution harness.

name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
weaknesses: Agentic coding trailed Claude
Occasional instruction-following lapses

name: Gemini 3 Pro
strengths: Record HLE and GPQA scores at launch
Deep Think mode for hardest problems
weaknesses: Premium long-context pricing tier
Deep Think gated behind Ultra subscription

name: Llama 3.1 405B
strengths: First open-weights model at GPT-4 level
weaknesses: Huge to serve
Text-only
notes: Pricing shown is typical hosted-inference cost.

name: Llama 4 Maverick
strengths: Very cheap to serve per token
weaknesses: Launch marred by LMArena benchmark controversy
Behemoth flagship repeatedly delayed
Fell behind Chinese open models within months
notes: Meta's last major open release before its superintelligence-lab reorg.

name: Grok 3
strengths: Strong math with Big Brain reasoning mode
weaknesses: Benchmark presentation drew methodology criticism
Limited availability at launch
notes: Reasoning-mode (beta) scores shown.

name: Grok 4
strengths: Led HLE and ARC-AGI-2 at launch
Heavy multi-agent variant for hardest problems
weaknesses: Launch overshadowed by Grok chatbot safety incidents
SWE-bench claims lacked independent verification at first

name: Grok 4.1
strengths: Much lower hallucination rate than Grok 4
weaknesses: Fewer published benchmark details than peers

name: Mistral Large 2
weaknesses: Research-only license for the weights
Behind US/China frontier on reasoning

name: Mistral Medium 3
strengths: 90% of Claude-Sonnet-class performance at 1/8 the price
weaknesses: Not open weights, breaking Mistral tradition
No frontier reasoning mode

name: DeepSeek-V3
weaknesses: Text-only
English polish behind US flagships

name: DeepSeek-R1
strengths: o1-class reasoning, fully open weights, MIT license
Triggered a $1T market repricing of AI costs
weaknesses: Verbose reasoning traces
Heavy censorship on China-sensitive topics
notes: The 'DeepSeek moment' of January 2025.

name: DeepSeek-V3.2
strengths: Cheapest capable reasoning API on the market
weaknesses: Slightly behind V3.1-Terminus on a few evals at launch
notes: Listed as V3.2-Exp at release.

name: Qwen3-235B-A22B
weaknesses: Agentic coding behind frontier closed models
notes: Scores for the July 2025 updated (2507) checkpoints where applicable.

name: Qwen3-Max
weaknesses: Closed weights, unlike the rest of the Qwen line

name: Kimi K2
strengths: 1T-parameter open MoE built for agentic tool use
Best open non-reasoning model at release
weaknesses: No reasoning mode at launch
Non-thinking AIME/HLE scores are low

name: Kimi K2 Thinking
strengths: Open-weights HLE record at release — beat GPT-5 on agentic HLE
weaknesses: Slow generation at high thinking budgets
INT4 serving needed for practical speed
notes: The strongest open reasoning model as of late 2025.

name: Kimi K3
strengths: Largest open-weight model at launch (2.8T total params, 16 of 896 experts active per token)
1M-token context window with native visual understanding
Leads open models on Moonshot's coding/agentic suite (Terminal-Bench 2.1 88.3, SWE Marathon 42.0) and tops LMArena's Frontend Code Arena at 1,679 Elo
weaknesses: Trails Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol on Moonshot's own evaluation suite
Only one reasoning-effort tier at launch, with reasoning token usage notably higher than K2.6
Full open-weight download delayed to July 27, 2026, about 11 days after API launch
notes: MMLU-Pro, AIME 2025, and SWE-bench Verified are unconfirmed or conflicting in third-party coverage (AIME cited as both 88% and 96.1%) so left null; Moonshot's own suite reports Terminal-Bench 2.1 at 88.3 and SWE Marathon at 42.0 instead. HLE is 43.5% no tools / 56.0% with tools; full open weights due 2026-07-27.

name: Mistral Large 3
strengths: Strongest Apache-2.0 open model from Europe
675B MoE with 41B active — cheap official API
weaknesses: Behind US/Chinese frontier flagships on raw capability
262K context trails 1M-class rivals
notes: Official pricing $0.50/$1.50; some trackers list hosted rates of $2/$6. GPQA Diamond 67.17 per Mistral's HF model card; not a dedicated reasoning model, so most 2026 reasoning benchmarks are unreported.

name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
strengths: ARC-AGI-2 and GPQA Diamond leader at release
2x+ reasoning gain over Gemini 3 Pro at unchanged pricing
weaknesses: Trails Claude on agentic coding and creative writing
Long-context pricing doubles above 200K tokens
notes: Preview Feb 11, 2026; ARC-AGI-2 reported as 77.1 (76.5 in some sources). Knowledge cutoff Jan 2025 per DeepMind model page. AIME 98.1 is vals.ai-measured (third-party); MMLU-Pro not publicly reported.

name: Qwen3.5
strengths: 397B MoE with only 17B active — very cheap to serve
weaknesses: Superseded within a quarter by Qwen3.6 and Qwen3.7 Max
notes: Figures are for the Qwen3.5-397B-A17B open release. MMLU-Pro 87.8 / GPQA 88.4 / SWE-bench 76.4 third-party (LLM-Stats/DeepInfra). Pricing is the DeepInfra hosted FP8 rate — no first-party API for the open weights.

name: Mistral Small 4
strengths: Unifies reasoning, vision and coding lines in one 119B/6.5B-active model
Among the cheapest multimodal reasoning models
weaknesses: Small-tier ceiling; not a frontier flagship
notes: 256k context and $0.15/$0.60 pricing per Mistral's announcement (earlier trackers listed 128k). MMLU-Pro 78.0 / GPQA 71.2 third-party-reported; LMArena Elo 1358 via BenchLM's Arena tracking.

name: Llama 5
strengths: Open weights at frontier scale (600B+, trained on 500K+ B200s)
weaknesses: Overshadowed by Meta's own closed Muse Spark, launched the same day
Sparse public benchmark documentation
notes: Meta published no standard benchmark table at launch and trackers had not evaluated it as of July 2026; open weights, so no first-party API pricing.

name: Muse Spark
strengths: First closed frontier model from Meta Superintelligence Labs
Free to consumers via the Meta AI app
weaknesses: Weak ARC-AGI-2 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro (42.5 vs 77.1)
No public API or pricing yet
notes: Replaced Llama as Meta's flagship line. Closed hosted model — no public API pricing or max-output figure. SWE-bench 77.4 lab-reported; HLE 58.4 is third-party (LLM-Stats), with tools in Contemplating mode; ARC-AGI-2 42.5 in Contemplating mode.

name: GPT-5.5
strengths: ARC-AGI-2 85% — best novel-pattern reasoning score reported
Terminal-Bench 2.0 state of the art at release
weaknesses: Trails Claude Opus on SWE-bench Pro and HLE without tools
Price doubled vs GPT-5.1
notes: GPT-5.5 Pro variant ($30/$180) sits #2 on LMArena. Context reported as 1M API / 1.1M by some trackers. Knowledge cutoff Dec 2025. SWE-bench Verified 88.7 lab-reported; LMArena Elo 1481 is the GPT-5.5 (high) listing.

name: DeepSeek-V4
strengths: Best open-weights coding model — SWE-bench Verified 80.6% (V4-Pro-Max)
~29x cheaper per output token than Claude Opus 4.8
1M context with 384K max output, MIT license
weaknesses: Serving capacity constrained by Huawei Ascend supply
No dedicated R2 reasoning release
notes: V4-Pro is a 1.6T/49B-active MoE; V4-Flash costs $0.14/$0.28. MMLU-Pro 87.5 / GPQA 90.1 are DeepSeek-reported for V4-Pro in Max reasoning mode; HLE 48.2 is third-party (LLM-Stats), same mode.

name: Grok 4.3
strengths: Cheapest near-frontier flagship after a ~40% price cut
First xAI model with native video input
weaknesses: Intelligence-index tier below the frontier leaders
Grok 5 (6T MoE) repeatedly delayed
notes: Requests above 200K tokens are billed at 2x. GPQA Diamond 90.1 per Artificial Analysis (third-party). xAI documents no fixed max-output cap; xAI published agentic (tau2-Bench, IFBench) rather than academic benchmarks at launch.

name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
strengths: Beats Gemini 3.1 Pro on coding/agentic suites at ~25% lower cost
Up to 4x faster than comparable frontier models
weaknesses: Flash-tier depth limits on hardest reasoning benchmarks
65K output cap
notes: Announced at Google I/O 2026; Terminal-Bench 2.1 76.2%. SWE-bench Verified 81.0 from I/O reporting; HLE 40.2 is third-party (LLM-Stats); ARC-AGI-2 72.1; LMArena Elo 1481 at launch window.

name: Qwen3.7 Max
strengths: Highest-ranked Chinese model ever on the Artificial Analysis index at launch
Built for long-horizon agent workflows with thousands of tool calls
weaknesses: Closed weights — a break from Qwen's open-source tradition
notes: SWE-bench Pro 60.6; Terminal-Bench 2.0 69.7. SWE-bench Verified 80.4 and HLE 41.4 lab/W&B-reported; MMLU-Pro 89.6 is third-party (LLM-Stats). Alibaba reports HMMT 97.1 instead of AIME.

name: Claude Opus 4.8
strengths: #1 on LMArena overall and coding at release
Proactively flags uncertainty about its own work
weaknesses: GPQA slightly below Opus 4.7 and Gemini 3.1 Pro
Reviewed as a modest step, not a leap
notes: HLE 49.8% no tools / 57.9% with tools. LMArena Elo corrected to 1484 (current Opus 4.8 Thinking listing, July 2026); an earlier 1510 figure did not match the live leaderboard.

name: Claude Fable 5
strengths: First generally available Mythos-class model — state of the art on nearly every tested benchmark
Days-long agentic autonomy (Stripe: 50M-line migration in one day)
State-of-the-art vision; rebuilds web apps from screenshots
weaknesses: Expensive at $10/$50 vs Opus 4.8's $5/$25
Safety classifiers reroute a small share of cyber/bio sessions to Opus 4.8
Launch controversy over silent throttling of suspected distillation
notes: Shares weights with the restricted Claude Mythos 5 (vetted partners only). HLE 64.5% is with tools; GPQA/SWE figures are third-party — Anthropic retired saturated benchmarks from official reporting.

name: Gemma 4
strengths: 31B dense flagship beats open models 10-20x its size on a meaningful share of published benchmarks
#3 open model on the LMArena text leaderboard at release
Native image, video and (on the E2B/E4B tiers) audio input across the whole family
Apache 2.0 license across all five sizes (E2B, E4B, 12B, 26B-A4B, 31B)
weaknesses: No SWE-bench or agentic-coding figures published at launch
256K context trails 1M-class open rivals like DeepSeek-V4 and Kimi K2 Thinking
No first-party hosted API pricing — open weights only
notes: Benchmarks shown are for the 31B Dense variant. LMArena Elo (~1452) and the MMLU-Pro/GPQA/AIME/HLE figures are third-party community-reported, not an official Google table. Weights were quietly published March 31, 2026; the formal Google blog announcement followed April 2, 2026.

name: GLM-5.2
strengths: Leading open-weights model on Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Index at release
Beat GPT-5.5 on SWE-bench Pro (62.1 vs 58.6) at roughly 1/6th the token cost
1M-token context via a new IndexShare sparse-attention scheme, cutting per-token FLOPs ~2.9x
MIT license with no regional restrictions
weaknesses: Text-only — no vision, unlike closed rivals
Shipped with no official benchmark suite; early scores were self-reported for six days before a verified table followed
ARC-AGI-2 22.8% — best of the open models but far behind closed frontier leaders
notes: SWE-bench Pro 62.1 reported; no SWE-bench Verified figure has been published. GPQA Diamond sources conflict (91.2 per the official Hugging Face model card vs ~80.3 in some third-party trackers) — official figure used. HLE 40.5% no tools / 54.7% with tools. Max output listed as 128K in most reporting; some sources cite up to 163,840 for extended reasoning.

name: GPT-5.6
strengths: Record GPQA Diamond (94.6%) and ARC-AGI-2 (92.5% at max reasoning effort) at release
OpenAI's strongest cybersecurity model yet — threat modeling, code review, blue-teaming
54% more token-efficient on coding tasks than GPT-5.5
Beats Claude Fable 5 on the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index at under half the output tokens
weaknesses: No SWE-bench Verified or AIME figures published at launch
Public rollout briefly delayed by a government review before the July 9 release
Sol's $5/$30 pricing sits above the mid-tier Terra and budget Luna variants
notes: Figures shown are for the Sol (frontier) tier of the three-model GPT-5.6 family; Terra ($2.50/$15) and Luna ($1/$6) are cheaper variants with lower Intelligence Index scores (55 and 51 vs Sol's 59). Context window and max output confirmed via OpenAI's developer docs. GPQA Diamond 94.6 and HLE 47.2 are OpenAI/Artificial Analysis-reported; MMLU-Pro 89.1 and ARC-AGI-2 92.5 (Max effort, per ARC Prize) are third-party. SWE-bench Verified and AIME were not published for this family.

name: Soofi S 30B-A3B
strengths: First model from Germany's sovereign SOOFI consortium (Fraunhofer IAIS/IIS, DFKI, TU Darmstadt and others)
Hybrid Mamba-2/MoE design activates only ~3.2B of 31.6B parameters per token, giving roughly 8x the throughput of dense 14-24B models at long context
Best open-model code aggregate in both English and German among 17 open base models tested
Trained end-to-end on Deutsche Telekom's sovereign Industrial AI Cloud in Munich
weaknesses: Released as a 'preview / internal checkpoint' — an unaligned base model, not intended for direct deployment
Matches dense 14-27B open models on aggregate benchmarks rather than pushing the open-weights frontier
License unfinalized at release; context window and standard reasoning benchmarks (MMLU-Pro, GPQA, SWE-bench, etc.) unpublished
notes: No MMLU-Pro/GPQA Diamond/SWE-bench/AIME/HLE/LMArena/ARC-AGI-2 scores have been published; reported metrics instead cover HumanEval (73.8%), MBPP (70.2%, 84.2% for the German variant) and aggregate English/German scores. Coordinated by the KI Bundesverband and funded under Germany's IPCEI-CIS program; arXiv preprint posted 2026-07-10, press release and Hugging Face weights 2026-07-13.
